AC: 10
STR: +3 DEX: +3 HP: +10
WT: 4.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL except DRU NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Iksar Scaled Gloves item window 2017

Post Comment
Iksar Scaled Gloves
QuoteReply
# Oct 03 2013 at 5:06 PM Rating: Good
snailish
4,580 posts
Dropped for me tonight off Grachnist the Destroyer (so as it always was known to be..).

This is the "junk" good drop from him, so you have a great chance of getting them if they are what you actually want.
▲ Page top
Iksar Scaled Gloves
QuoteReply
# Sep 09 2017 at 6:09 AM Rating: Excellent
snailish
4,580 posts
snailish wrote:
Dropped for me tonight off Grachnist the Destroyer (so as it always was known to be..).

This is the "junk" good drop from him, so you have a great chance of getting them if they are what you actually want.


On progression servers where the goblins are being farmed heavily, these are rotting. They are super common --so no reason to spend big $ on them in commonlands or baz (if your server is that far).

i saw
QuoteReply
# Aug 02 2003 at 1:23 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592760461953
48 posts
i bought these for 13pp from a vendor in the bazaar--- was pretty happy
▲ Page top
Very affordable gloves
QuoteReply
# Jul 07 2002 at 5:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759517522
21 posts
These gloves may not be the best, but for the price they are some of the best you can find. Personally I haven't bought them, but in the Rallos Zek auctions, you can commonly see them go for around 50pp. These gloves drop EVERY time off the named goblin in the warsliks woods- I'm not sure what his level is but thats easy to look up, I'm sure hes an easy kill if you are 30ish. So, while you are saving up for those silver chitin hand wraps or crystal chitin gauntlets, allow me to recommend these as an affordable, dispensible temporary.
▲ Page top
RE: Very affordable gloves
QuoteReply
# Apr 11 2003 at 5:52 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250166807
Scholar
69 posts
A couple years ago, I was making a MINT off of these gloves. High level PCs were camping grach for the earring or whatever, and since these were lore I could loot them at my leisure. Then I'd port out to EC and sell them for a couple hundred pp. Made some nice cash doing that back in the day when these were brandy new.
As far as grach goes, he's actually pretty tough. And his fort is full of potential adds. Every gob in the fort is a PH for him, and I've seen more than 1 grach at a time on track.

Even though the 3+ dex hits more, unless your group has a rouge and a warrior tank, you probably dont want these (unless you wanna twink a monk)

QuoteReply
# Dec 16 2001 at 12:46 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757281072
View User Forum
44 posts
There is a mistake in the listings. This can also be worn by Vah Shir Beastlords. I know my young Beastlord Cat "Tumbi" is wearing them. I had trouble selling them and my Barbarians could not wear em. They are great for her current level.
▲ Page top
AC vs Dex (and str)
QuoteReply
# Nov 14 2001 at 5:09 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591769261394
View User Forum
34 posts
A good question you should ask yourself is, when in a group with other tanks, in general, am I the guy getting hit more than anyone else? That is, are the targets generally taunting on you more than the other tanks. If yes, than you are probably doing more damage than the other tanks, and subsequently you should probably concentrate on more AC. If the answer is no, than you could probably benefit more from anything that would increase your damage output. Of course this is just a rule of thumb, and the situation does change when you are soloing.
▲ Page top
this is how i see it
QuoteReply
# Nov 14 2001 at 10:34 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591764371556
25 posts
the more damage you deal, and the faster you do so, the quicker the kill and the less you have to worry about AC. granted, no matter how much haste, dex, and str you have, you're going to get hit if you melee at all. now the question is, which has a more noticeable effect in combat: 2 less ac, or 3 more dex?
▲ Page top
RE: this is how i see it
QuoteReply
# Nov 14 2001 at 10:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247010486
Scholar
360 posts
I would expect that 3 DEX would give ZERO noticeable effect whatsoever, EVEN IF you had 2 proc weapons going. I have heard people complain that Boon of the Garou (the Enchanter spell, grants Werewolf form and +100 DEX) hardly affects their proc rate at all.

Never experienced it myself - just reporting observations that I have heard.

Arynduil Greyleaf
Ranger of Tunare
EMarr
▲ Page top
RE: this is how i see it
QuoteReply
# Jun 16 2003 at 8:48 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592341440538
Sage
312 posts
those observations you heard are extremely bogus
i notice a considerable difference in proc rate when i get a dex buff
+100 would scream at you, the change of rate would definitely be noticable
but +3 ... no probably not going to notice
▲ Page top
Iksar Scaled vs. Ry'gorr
QuoteReply
# Jun 16 2001 at 2:42 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247003324
47 posts
These gloves are nice, and much better and more cost efficient when compared to the Ry'gorr Chain gloves, which are more expensive and give less strength and only 2 more ac (not to mention the dex stats this gives). I'd highly recommend this over Ry'gorr.

RE: Iksar Scaled vs. Ry'gorr
QuoteReply
# Sep 09 2001 at 7:59 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765385143
48 posts
Aye Ac is nice, but stats have their place. One one hand I think about all the rangers, rogues and bards out there. They tend to use a lot of procing weapons (dex), and are typically not the main tank of the group. 5 dex versus 2 ac, more damage for thicker skin. Both points are valid, but I opt for the risk of dealing more damage.
▲ Page top
RE: Iksar Scaled vs. Ry'gorr
QuoteReply
# Jan 18 2002 at 5:39 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591755789514
61 posts
Ac is secondary to rogues, you want as much STR you can get for that damage. Dex helps as well for procs, although its not extremely noticeable.

Grahcnist
QuoteReply
# May 26 2001 at 9:03 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754129614
3 posts
He does Have a lot of HP, but watch out for other goblins healing him. This happened when I was helping a guild mate get a pair of the gloves
▲ Page top
Gloves
QuoteReply
# May 23 2001 at 3:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591755346357
4 posts
I went there with my druid friend (L34 ranger and druid) and we did fine... most goblins are green. Grachnid was on track almost immediately when we entered. I tracked him down and defeated him (he's tough tho, don't try to solo!)

I needed another pair for a guild mate, so I started camping the goblin ruins and killing gobies, half an hour later he spawned again and he dropped the gloves both times.

Check out my story on it here:
http://pub65.ezboard.com/fepicheroes73161frm1


Safe way to do this:
Enter zone from OT.
Go southwest untill you see the path.
Follow the path and go left at intersection.
you'll pass some ruins with iksar, continue.
Now you come to 2nd ruins with gobies.
I camped to the left of the path and kep killing gobbies till Grachnid spawned.
▲ Page top
RE: Gloves
QuoteReply
# Jul 22 2001 at 4:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245547813
10 posts
Bah, he's way easy. I came in with my 35 druid and he just happened to be there. Didn't even know who he was, but he was blue so I pulled him. Snare him, get him away from the others so they can't heal him, then root him. Drones and Immo then med. Repeat as necessary. He stayed rooted quite well, never even got a hit on me. Only dropped the gloves...

▲ Page top
wuh
QuoteReply
# Apr 24 2001 at 9:21 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
wait, y r these worth LESS than gem inlaid gaunts? they have more AC and str, and the same dex. only difference is the 3 less sta, but most melee want str more than sta. maybe its just me
▲ Page top
RE: wuh
QuoteReply
# May 16 2001 at 5:45 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247242173
73 posts
But if you are not a monk and can use these, DEFINTELY get these instead Gem Inlaid Gauntlets. The +3 Str will let you carry more, netting only a 1.0 weight. Plus the Str will help you with your damage output.

Bottom line: same net weight, more Str, more AC, much cheaper, but less Sta.
▲ Page top
RE: wuh
QuoteReply
# Oct 24 2001 at 3:55 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Actually, the +3str won't let you carry more, because this weighs 4. Your carrying capacity actually goes down by one with this. If you're replacing gloves that weigh 1.0, these have no net change on your carrying capacity.

I'd agree with you that these are better than gem inlaid gauntlets, if weight isn't an issue. But if you're a monk, or a class that can't use these, gem inlaid gauntlets are much better.

The reason that these are cheaper than gem inlaid, despite the fact that it has more AC, is mainly for two reasons:
1. Gem inlaid gauntlets can be used by all classes, all races. Since more people can wear them, there's a bigger demand.

2. These drop off a single mob, and it's his common drop. Gem inlaid gauntlets are the result of a quest that requires 4 different frops off of mobs. That means these are a lot easier to get, and more common.

So basic economics: lower supply, higher demand, means that gem inlaid sells for more than these do.
▲ Page top
RE: wuh
QuoteReply
# Apr 25 2001 at 3:36 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592791513703
View User Forum
Scholar
229 posts
The difference is the weight, and who can use them. Monks, casters and all large races, will still want gem inlaid gauntlets, so juts because these are cheap and easy to get, it does not really drive down the price of gem inlaid gauntlets.
▲ Page top
Bad boy
QuoteReply
# Apr 19 2001 at 3:47 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Found this guy the other nite.
Was helping a very low level Iksar monk get his corpse back and log in my high level Sk(witch I took all of his gear..minus the no drop stuff and started up a iksar sk). So thier I was at 42..equpt with a Sarnak Berzerk whip 5/27,a Marine shield 8 +10 vs magic,Cougar skin mask 6 2 dex 2 agil 10 hps,shin greeves and shin gauntlets..ageast Grachnist.

It was a tuff fight thats for sure. He can hit in the 90s. Of course that might have been because my AC was only like 580 or so. I must say for a blue he does have a **** load of hps. I layed into him with harm touch (441 dmg)..throw Boil blood,engulfing darkness,Heart flutter..recasted Engulfing darkness.(was keping him feared since I know I didn't have the weapons or armor to take him Toe to Toe). Was looking realy bad..he was getting close to dead and he beat me down to 2 bubbles of health, when I broke him moral and he ran for it..well walked since darkness.

To bad..he only had his gloves.
▲ Page top

Anonymous
